monsters.province_origin: Maranao and wider Mindanao storytelling traditions
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Maranao oral literature and trickster tale traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Small Wit, Great Victory — Gains increased evasion and Luck against larger enemies. Once per battle, may avoid a fatal hit through a trick escape.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Riddle Under the Throne — Confuses one enemy with a clever riddle, lowering accuracy and stealing one minor buff.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Mouse-Deer Escape Plan — Becomes Hidden, cleanses self of Root or Slow, and grants Haste to allies after escaping.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Confuse, Haste, Evasive Step, Trick Path, Taunt, Mark Prey, Calm, Reveal, Quick Strike, Smoke Veil, Weaken, Barrier, Counter Ready, Ancestral Guard, Hopeful Hymn
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires winning a trick contest without cheating or humiliating the weak
monsters.habitat: Highland Village, Bamboo Thicket, Market Town, Rice Terraces, Spirit Threshold
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Forest fruit, rice cake, riddle token, small brass bell, woven pouch
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Pilandok is celebrated in many Mindanao stories as a small but clever trickster who defeats larger, richer, or more powerful opponents through wit. In ALAMAT, Pilandok is not a monster in the frightening sense but a Nilalang folk hero whose stories have become so widely retold that his spirit walks between tale and reality. He appears where arrogant rulers, greedy merchants, or cruel strongmen believe size and power make them untouchable. The player encounters Pilandok during a village contest where the wealthy have rigged the rules against ordinary farmers. To earn his trust, the player must solve riddles, expose the rigged game, and win without becoming cruel in return. Once bonded, Pilandok becomes an evasive trickster support who confuses enemies, escapes traps, steals minor buffs, and turns unfair battles into contests of cleverness.
monsters.folklore_summary: Pilandok is a clever mouse-deer trickster figure associated especially with Maranao storytelling. Tales often show him outwitting kings, crocodiles, or stronger opponents through intelligence and humor.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT treats Pilandok as a story-born Nilalang whose power grows wherever the powerless use wit against arrogance.
monsters.physical_description: A small mouse-deer spirit with bright clever eyes, tan-brown fur, tiny curved horns, a colorful woven sash, small brass bell, and a satchel of riddle tokens. Its shadow sometimes forms exaggerated heroic poses.
monsters.weaknesses: Silence, honest humility, unavoidable area attacks, anti-trickster seals, broken riddles
monsters.resistances: Trickster, Confusion, Taunt, Root escape, fear from larger enemies
monsters.npc_related: Datu Salin the judge; Bai Amirah the storyteller; Pilo the cheated farmer
monsters.boss_variant: Pilandok ng Pitong Palaisipan — creates layered riddle phases and decoy targets.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Pilandok ng Mapanlinlang na Tawa — uses wit only to humiliate and steal luck.
monsters.divine_variant: Pilandok, Gabay ng Matalino at Mababa — radiant trickster who protects the humble.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A quick clever chirp followed by tiny hoof taps and a mischievous laugh.
monsters.drop_items: Riddle Token, Mouse-Deer Bell, Clever Sash Thread, Lucky Hoofprint
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bell, Beadwork, Woven Garb, Anting-Anting, Spirit Mask
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Pilandok inspired by Maranao trickster tales: small mouse-deer spirit, tan-brown fur, bright clever eyes, tiny horns, colorful woven sash, brass bell, riddle-token satchel, playful pose, transparent background, readable chibi RPG trickster style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Pilandok, clever mouse-deer folk hero from Mindanao storytelling, bright eyes, woven sash, tiny horns, brass bell, village contest background, charming and witty RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Pilandok hops onto a tiny stage, flips a riddle token, enemy symbols tangle into knots, then Pilandok vanishes behind a woven sash and reappears with stolen buff light.
monsters.design_notes: Use as a heroic trickster, not a scary monster. Great for non-combat problem-solving mechanics.

monsters.province_origin: Bicol river, lake, and swamp tale regions
monsters.passive_ability: Mud-Scale Bulk — Gains Defense when Soaked or in River habitats. Immune to forced movement from weaker enemies.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Swamp Maw Slam — Surges from mud and bites one enemy, dealing River/Beast damage and applying Soaked.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Old Lake Tremor — Thrashes its massive body, damaging all enemies and lowering Speed through muddy shockwaves.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Water Strike, Heavy Strike, Guard, Fortify, Soaked, Slow, Earth Slam, Bite, Taunt, Counter Ready, Stone Skin, Protect Ally, Regeneration, River Flow, Crushing Grip
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires ending greedy netting and restoring the swamp's spawning grounds
monsters.habitat: Hidden Lake, Riverbank, Mangrove Marsh, Waterfall Sanctuary, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.weather_preference: Rain, Heavy Rain, Fog, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Freshwater shrimp, swamp lotus, clean mud bowl, repaired net, river-stone charm
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Kugtong is remembered in Bicolano folklore as a great fish of extraordinary size and appetite. In ALAMAT, Kugtong are old swamp guardians who swallow not only prey but also broken things the river wants hidden: rusted hooks, cursed coins, poisoned bait, and forgotten oaths. This Kugtong blocks a lake passage after merchants drag spawning grounds with heavy nets, killing young fish and stirring old mud. The player must repair the breeding pools, remove iron hooks from the lakebed, and feed the Kugtong only what the river can spare. Once bonded, Kugtong becomes a slow but powerful tank with high defense, water control, and mud-based battlefield disruption. It teaches that abundance is not permission to take everything.
monsters.folklore_summary: Kugtong is a giant fish figure in Bicolano folklore, commonly described as enormous and dangerous. It appears in regional tales involving rivers, lakes, or deep waters.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Kugtong as a swamp and lake guardian that punishes greed and protects spawning grounds.
monsters.physical_description: A massive mud-brown freshwater fish with mossy scales, whisker-like barbels, glowing green eyes, a wide ancient mouth, stone-like armor plates, and lotus roots tangled around its fins.
monsters.weaknesses: Lightning, drought, poisoned bait, overfishing, exposed dry ground
monsters.resistances: River, Earth, Soaked, Slow, physical impact, knockback
monsters.npc_related: Ka Ambo the fisherman; Luningning the lake diver; Datu Baybay
monsters.boss_variant: Kugtong ng Lumang Lawa — changes water depth and creates mud walls.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Kugtong ng Lason na Putik — spreads poison mud with each thrash.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Pangingitlugan — cleansed spawning-ground guardian with lotus light.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A deep bubbling bellow from under mud, followed by a huge water slap.
monsters.drop_items: Mud Scale Plate, Lotus Root Fin, Old Hook Iron, Swamp Pearl
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Shell Charm, Totem, Relic Weapon, Herbal Bundle, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Kugtong inspired by Bicolano folklore: enormous mud-brown freshwater fish guardian, mossy scales, barbels, glowing green eyes, stone armor plates, lotus roots around fins, transparent background, readable chibi RPG tank silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Kugtong, giant Bicolano swamp fish, mossy mud scales, ancient mouth, green glowing eyes, lotus swamp background, massive but protective RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Kugtong's glowing eyes appear under muddy water, the swamp bulges upward, a huge fish maw erupts, then a body slam sends muddy waves across the battlefield.
monsters.design_notes: Should feel ancient and massive. Strong environmental conservation quest potential.

monsters.province_origin: Maranao epic tradition regions around Lake Lanao
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Maranao epic literature, especially Darangen-associated monster traditions
monsters.passive_ability: Many-Limbed Threat — Can strike an additional random enemy after using a physical skill. Takes reduced damage from single-target control effects.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Tentacle Storm Grasp — Strikes multiple enemies with whipping limbs, dealing Sea/Beast damage and applying Marked to the front row.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Black Reef Devour — Pulls enemies toward a dark reef maw, dealing heavy Shadow/Sea damage and applying Fear.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Water Strike, Heavy Strike, Fear Cry, Mark Prey, Crushing Grip, Soaked, Guard Break, Counter Ready, Dark Pulse, Ambush, Sea Roar, Taunt, Tsunami, Weaken, Reckless Charge
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires surviving an epic trial and freeing a reef from war-memory corruption
monsters.habitat: Open Sea, Coral Reef, Storm Coast, Spirit Threshold, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.weather_preference: Typhoon, Heavy Rain, Eclipse, Spirit Glow
monsters.favorite_food_offering: War drum rhythm, black coral charm, clean reef water, shell bell, hero's vow
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Kurita is adapted for ALAMAT from epic monster traditions associated with Maranao heroic literature, where heroes face terrible beings of overwhelming force. In the game world, Kurita is a many-limbed sea horror born from war memories trapped in coral and stormwater. It does not simply hunt; it reenacts old battles, dragging ships and warriors into a reef that remembers violence. The player encounters Kurita during a storm trial where the sea repeats an ancient conflict in fragments. To earn its trust, the player must stop feeding the reef with vengeance, recover broken war drums, and complete a hero's vow without glorifying bloodshed. Once bonded, Kurita becomes a brutal berserker that attacks multiple targets, marks frontlines, and turns battlefield pressure into overwhelming force.
monsters.folklore_summary: Kurita appears in discussions of Maranao epic monster traditions, especially in relation to heroic battles in the Darangen cycle and related retellings. ALAMAT adapts it as a many-limbed sea monster archetype.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Kurita as a sea monster formed from war-memory corruption, requiring courage and restraint to redeem.
monsters.physical_description: A huge dark sea monster with multiple tentacle-like limbs, jagged coral armor, a central maw glowing blue-black, storm-slick hide, and old war-drum patterns marked across its body.
monsters.personality: Aggressive, Restless, Vengeful, Honorable when calmed
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred shell bells, calm rituals, Sun after storm, broken vengeance cycles, lightning overexposure
monsters.resistances: Sea, Shadow, Fear, knockback, single-target root, physical pressure
monsters.npc_related: Bai Amirah the epic singer; Datu Salin; Ka Ramil the drum maker
monsters.boss_variant: Kurita ng Itim na Bahura — attacks with many limbs and reef-mouth hazards.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Kurita ng Walang Katapusang Digmaan — repeats battle phases endlessly until vows are broken.
monsters.divine_variant: Kurita, Bantay ng Payapang Bahura — calmed reef guardian with silver coral armor.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A deep sea roar mixed with underwater war drums and cracking coral.
monsters.drop_items: Black Coral Plate, War-Drum Barnacle, Many-Limb Sinew, Storm Reef Shard
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Shell Charm, Relic Weapon, Instrument, Totem, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Kurita inspired by Maranao epic sea monster traditions: huge many-limbed dark sea horror, tentacle-like arms, jagged coral armor, blue-black glowing central maw, stormwater aura, war-drum markings, transparent background, readable chibi RPG boss silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Kurita, many-limbed sea monster inspired by Mindanao epic traditions, dark reef body, coral armor, glowing maw, storm waves and war drum motifs, epic RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Kurita rises from a black reef, multiple limbs slam the field in sequence, war-drum shockwaves mark enemies, then a blue-black maw drags the front row into a fear burst.
monsters.design_notes: Use as epic monster, not generic kraken. Tie to war memory, restraint, and Maranao-inspired epic scale respectfully.

monsters.province_origin: Maranao epic tradition regions around Lake Lanao
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Maranao epic literature and oral tradition
monsters.passive_ability: Broken-Oath Rage — Gains Attack whenever an enemy breaks a buff, shield, or vow-type effect. Corrupted Tarabusaw also gains Frenzy at low HP.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Oath-Crushing Fist — Smashes one enemy with Mountain/Beast damage and removes one buff or shield.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Giant's Broken Challenge — Forces all enemies to face it, applying Taunted and Weakened while increasing Tarabusaw's attack.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heavy Strike, Earth Slam, Taunt, Guard Break, Reckless Charge, Stomp, Fortify, Stone Skin, Fear Cry, Counter Ready, Weaken, Frenzy, Protect Ally, Mountain Crush, Dark Pulse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires completing a duel of honor and restoring a broken oath stone
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Highland Village, Spirit Threshold, Corrupted Wilds, Abandoned Watchtower
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Oath stone, roasted root crops, battle drum rhythm, mountain salt, repaired shield
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Tarabusaw is adapted in ALAMAT as an epic giant adversary connected to broken oaths, heroic duels, and the dangers of strength without honor. The creature appears after a mountain clan breaks an old peace agreement and blames the enemy village for the resulting disasters. Tarabusaw rises from the cracked oath stone, challenging everyone to battle because it no longer recognizes truthful speech. To earn trust, the player must restore the oath stone, uncover the lie that broke the pact, and defeat Tarabusaw in a duel where using deception causes immediate failure. Once bonded, Tarabusaw becomes a berserker who breaks shields, forces enemies into direct confrontation, and grows stronger when vows or buffs are broken.
monsters.folklore_summary: Tarabusaw appears in Maranao epic-associated monster traditions and heroic tales, often as a powerful adversary. ALAMAT adapts the figure as an oath-giant tied to duels and broken agreements.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT uses Tarabusaw as a giant born from broken mountain oaths, combining epic combat with moral accountability.
monsters.physical_description: A towering giant with dark stone-brown skin, cracked oath-stone plates across the chest, tusk-like teeth, massive fists, mountain-cloth wraps, and red-orange corruption lines glowing through old battle scars.
monsters.personality: Proud, Aggressive, Honorable when restored, Vengeful
monsters.weaknesses: Honest duel rites, restored oath stones, Sacred light, calm effects, trickery used against itself
monsters.resistances: Mountain, Beast, Taunt, Fear, physical damage, knockback
monsters.npc_related: Datu Salin; Ka Ramil the shield maker; Bai Amirah the epic singer
monsters.boss_variant: Tarabusaw ng Basag na Panata — enters rage phases whenever shields break.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Tarabusaw na Walang Dangal — destroys all vows and gains endless Frenzy.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Matapat na Laban — restored oath-giant who protects fair duels.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A booming challenge like rocks splitting across a mountain pass.
monsters.drop_items: Oath-Stone Plate, Giant Knuckle Shard, Broken Shield Iron, Duel Drum Skin
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Relic Weapon, Totem, Woven Garb, Bell, Anting-Anting
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Tarabusaw inspired by Mindanao epic monster traditions: towering oath-breaking giant, dark stone-brown skin, cracked oath-stone chest plates, huge fists, mountain wraps, glowing red-orange cracks, transparent background, readable chibi RPG berserker silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Tarabusaw, epic Mindanao giant adversary, cracked oath-stone armor, huge fists, red-orange battle scars, mountain storm background, proud and intimidating RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Tarabusaw slams an oath stone into the ground, enemy shields crack, a giant fist descends with mountain force, then a roar taunts all enemies into direct combat.
monsters.design_notes: Good for honor-duel quest mechanics. Avoid making it mindless; its rage comes from broken agreements.

monsters.passive_ability: Shadow Over the Ridge — At the start of battle, may Blind enemies if weather is Storm, Fog, or Eclipse.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Cliff-Shadow Dive — Dives from above, dealing Sky/Shadow damage and applying Blind.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Wingstorm Carry-Off — Creates a violent gust that damages all enemies and may move them to random positions.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Wind Slash, Evasive Flight, Blind, Haste, Storm Call, Mark Prey, Feather Guard, Fear Cry, Quick Slash, Ambush, Thunder Strike, Smoke Veil, Counter Ready, Reveal, Sky Guard
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires climbing a storm cliff and returning a stolen egg-shell relic
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Storm Coast, Abandoned Watchtower, Spirit Threshold, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Storm feather, wind bell, cliff fruit, clean water, egg-shell relic
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Pah is adapted in ALAMAT as a colossal sky bird from epic monster traditions of Mindanao, a being whose shadow can cover a ridge and whose wings can turn a safe climb into a storm trial. This Pah nests on cliffs where wind bells mark safe passage. When raiders steal an egg-shell relic from the nest, storms begin striking every path below. The player must climb during dangerous winds, repair the bells, and return the relic without taking a feather as trophy. Once bonded, Pah becomes an aerial ranger focused on Blind, forced positioning, and storm mobility. It is not as cosmic as Minokawa, but it is one of the most feared sky predators of the high cliffs.
monsters.folklore_summary: Pah is adapted here from Mindanao epic monster-bird traditions and giant bird motifs found in heroic narratives. ALAMAT uses it as a cliff and storm predator distinct from Minokawa and Sarimanok.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Pah as a storm-cliff guardian bird whose shadow warns against theft, arrogance, and dangerous climbs.
monsters.physical_description: A massive dark-feathered raptor with storm-gray wings, black crest, silver lightning streaks, hooked obsidian beak, talons like curved blades, and a shadow that spreads wider than its body.
monsters.weaknesses: Grounding seals, broken wind bells, Sun after storm, returned egg-shell rites, net traps
monsters.resistances: Sky, Storm, Shadow, Blind, forced movement, fear
monsters.npc_related: Ka Ramil the wind-bell maker; Apo Tala the sky watcher; Berto the cliff raider
monsters.boss_variant: Pah ng Itim na Bangin — carries enemies between cliff platforms and creates blind zones.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Pah ng Bagyong Walang Dulo — spreads endless storm and loses nest restraint.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Kampanang-Hangin — cleansed cliff bird with silver bell feathers.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A piercing raptor scream stretched through storm wind and ringing cliff bells.
monsters.drop_items: Storm Feather, Cliff Talon, Wind-Bell Shard, Shadow Crest Plume
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Feather Charm, Bell, Relic Weapon, Anting-Anting, Beadwork
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of Pah, a Mindanao epic-inspired giant sky bird: massive dark raptor, storm-gray wings, black crest, silver lightning streaks, obsidian beak, blade talons, wide shadow aura, transparent background, readable chibi RPG ranger silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of Pah, giant storm-cliff bird, dark feathers, lightning streaks, obsidian beak, enormous wings casting a shadow over cliffs, wind bells and storm clouds, epic RPG bestiary art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Pah's shadow covers the battlefield, wind bells ring violently, it dives from storm clouds with black wings spread, then gusts enemies into new positions with a wingstorm.
monsters.design_notes: Keep distinct from Minokawa: Pah is storm-cliff predator; Minokawa is celestial eclipse bird.
